title: Unknown catch-all variants serialize as literal unknown not original wire value
description: Six enums (ChatKind, DeliveryState, OwnerPresence, ChatMemberRole, ChannelPermissionTargetType, ContactPresence) use #[serde(other)] Unknown variants. When serialized, Unknown round-trips as the string 'unknown' rather than the original wire value. Any code path that deserializes a server object then re-serializes and re-transmits it (e.g. a relay, cached update, or forwarded message) will corrupt the field. The behavior is documented in a comment and test but the type system provides no warning. Maintenance consequence: contributors adding serialization paths will not discover this until a real server rejects 'unknown' as an invalid enum value.
